topic.what: Coordinated cross-modal attack that spoofs both camera and LiDAR to create spatially-consistent phantom objects bypassing multi-sensor fusion redundancy
topic.how: Synchronizes IR laser projection (adversarial camera patch) with LiDAR point injection, using extrinsic calibration to project fake 3D points onto spoofed 2D pixels in fused perception pipeline
topic.why_matters: Exposes critical vulnerability in MSF fusion logic; demonstrates that fabricating cross-sensor consistency (not inconsistency) bypasses redundancy checks that modern AVs rely on for safety

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of augmented scenes where phantom object detected with confidence >0.5 and correct location
characteristics.vs_baseline: No baseline; first demonstration of coordinated cross-modal attack
characteristics.context: Large-scale evaluation on PointPillars early-fusion perception model

characteristics.what_itasures: Confidence score assigned by detector to phantom objects
characteristics.value: Vehicle mean 0.75 median 0.79; Pedestrian mean 0.56 median 0.56
characteristics.evidence: Table I reports confidence distributions for both object classes
characteristics.context: High confidence scores indicate strong deception of 3D detector

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate at which phantom objects incorrectly detected as real
characteristics.context: High FPR demonstrates vulnerability of fusion logic

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of KITTI scenes used for evaluation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Largest evaluation for coordinated MSF attack
characteristics.evidence: Section IV-B describes augmentation pipeline and scene count
characteristics.context: Comprehensive evaluation covering two object classes at random 20-40m distances

characteristics.what_it_measures: Physical distance from AV where phantom objects injected
characteristics.evidence: Section IV-B specifies random plausible locations 20-40m in front of AV
characteristics.context: Targets practical attack scenarios on road

requires: black-box access to perception (no internal model knowledge)

novelty: First coordinated cross-modal attack fabricates sensor consistency (not inconsistency) to bypass MSF redundancy
Formal threat model for synchronized IR laser + LiDAR spoofing to create spatially-aligned phantom objects
Outcome-equivalent digital simulation isolating fusion logic vulnerability from physical attack complexity
Large-scale empirical validation (400 scenes) demonstrating 85.5% attack success rate on state-of-the-art perception
open_problems: Physical hardware implementation: IR laser projector + LiDAR signal injector synchronization not built
Physics-based environmental rendering needed to model ambient light surface reflectivity atmospheric interference
Real-world vehicle testing required to validate attack feasibility in dynamic driving conditions
Adaptive attack strategies for moving targets and ego-motion compensation
Defense development: fusion algorithms resilient to coordinated consistency attacks not yet explored
Temporal synchronization across sensor fusion cycles (10-30Hz) in physical deployment not validated
